seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with LlamaIndex + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with LlamaIndex + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: LlamaIndex simplifies building RAG pipelines by connecting LLMs to external data through indexing, retrieval, and synthesis. However, common pitfalls like irrelevant retrievals, hallucinations, and outdated indexes can undermine reliability in production. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure source faithfulness, context relevance, and citation accuracy for trustworthy responses.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with LlamaIndex
architectureOverview.description:
A typical LlamaIndex RAG pipeline starts with document ingestion, where raw data is chunked and embedded into a vector index like VectorStoreIndex. Queries trigger vector similarity search to retrieve top-k relevant chunks, which are then assembled into a prompt context. The LLM synthesizer generates responses with source attribution, citing specific nodes from retrieved documents.

Key components include the Index (storing embeddings), Retriever (finding relevant chunks), and QueryEngine (orchestrating retrieval + synthesis). Metadata filtering and reranking can enhance retrieval quality. The pipeline's reliability hinges on embedding quality, chunking strategy, and LLM faithfulness to provided context.

risks.name: Retrieved context irrelevant to query
risks.description: Vector search returns semantically distant chunks due to poor embeddings or query mismatch
risks.mitigation: Verify context relevance scores and force re-retrieval on low-confidence matches

risks.name: Synthesizer hallucinating beyond source documents
risks.description: LLM generates facts not grounded in retrieved context, eroding trust
risks.mitigation: Runtime hallucination detection comparing response claims to source chunks

risks.name: Index staleness causing outdated responses
risks.description: Documents change but index isn't refreshed, serving obsolete information
risks.mitigation: Timestamp verification and index refresh triggers with content drift detection

risks.name: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
risks.description: Document split at semantic boundaries splits key information across chunks
risks.mitigation: Validate cross-chunk context continuity and citation completeness

implementation.title: Basic VectorStoreIndex Setup
implementation.description: Create a simple RAG index from documents with default embedding model
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader

documents = SimpleDirectoryReader('data/').load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine()

response = query_engine.query('What is the main topic?')
print(response)

implementation.title: Query Engine with Citations
implementation.description: Enable source attribution for response traceability
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import Settings

query_engine = index.as_query_engine(
    similarity_top_k=3,
    response_mode='compact',
    node_postprocessors=[
        CitationQueryEngine(
            citation_chunk_size=512,
        )
    ]
)

response = query_engine.query('Summarize key findings')
print(response.get_formatted_sources())

implementation.title: TypeScript Index Creation
implementation.description: LlamaIndex TS setup with local embeddings

implementation.title: Metadata Filtering
implementation.code:
query_engine = index.as_query_engine(filters=MetadataFilters(
    filter=MetadataFilter(
        key='category', 
        value='finance'
    )
))

response = query_engine.query('Financial metrics?')
print(response)
